Percentage.text: A server has 128GB RAM. If 25% is used by the OS, how much RAM is free for applications?
A database grew from 200GB to 250GB. What is the percentage increase?
After fixing 40% of reported bugs, 75 bugs remain. How many bugs were initially reported?
A code module was optimized, reducing its size from 80KB to 60KB. What is the percentage reduction?
An app has 5000 users. 20% are active daily, and 10% of active users upgrade to premium. How many premium active users are there?
A 1TB drive has 350GB used. What percentage of the drive is free?
A server's response time improved by 20%, from 250ms. What is the new response time?
If 15% of a cloud cluster's instances are for staging, and there are 30 staging instances, how many total instances are there?
A data transfer rate increased by 10%, then decreased by 10%. If the original rate was 100Mbps, what is the final rate?
Out of 1200 packets, 450 are UDP. What percentage of packets are TCP?
A project has 60% test coverage for 200 lines of code. How many more lines need to be covered to reach 75% coverage?
40% of an application's bugs are critical. Of these critical bugs, 25% are P1 priority. What percentage of total bugs are P1 critical?
Server A had 99.9% uptime, Server B had 99.5% uptime. In a month (30 days), how many more minutes of downtime did Server B have than Server A?
The cost of cloud storage increased by 15% to $115 per month. What was the original cost?
After optimization, CPU utilization dropped by 20% to 60%. What was the utilization before optimization?
A system's performance score is 40% based on CPU (score 80) and 60% based on RAM (score 90). What is the overall score?
A user deletes 30% of their 500GB data. Then, they delete 20% of the remaining data. How much data is left?
In a code review, 3 out of 8 files had issues. What percentage of files had issues?
A datacenter upgrade reduced power consumption from 1200KW to 1080KW. What is the percentage saving?
A system processes 250,000 requests per hour. If the error rate is 0.02%, how many errors occur per hour?
A dev team has 6 backend, 4 frontend, and 2 DevOps engineers. What percentage of the team are frontend engineers?
A component's price increased by 20%, then decreased by 10%, ending at $216. What was the original price?
A project budget of $150,000 allocates 30% for hardware and 40% for software licenses. How much money is left for development?
A 500MB file is compressed to 350MB. What percentage of the original size is the compressed file?
In a server farm, 20% run Linux, 45% run Windows. The rest run MacOS. What percentage run MacOS?
A server is 60% utilized. If its capacity is 1000 processes, how many processes are running?
A 500 MB file is compressed by 20%. What is its new size in MB?
Out of 200 data packets transmitted, 5 were corrupted. What is the error percentage?
An algorithm's execution time decreased from 500ms to 400ms after optimization. What is the percentage improvement?
A system has 32 GB RAM. If 8 GB is currently in use, what percentage of RAM is free?
A web service currently uses 10 servers. Due to increased traffic, it needs to increase its capacity by 30%. How many additional servers are needed?
A database grew from 200 GB to 250 GB in a month. What is the percentage increase?
A software license costs $1500. A bulk purchase offers a 15% discount. What is the discounted price?
A server's CPU load dropped from 80% to 60%. What is the percentage decrease relative to the initial load?
A network connection has a bandwidth of 1 Gbps. If 250 Mbps is currently used, what percentage of bandwidth is free?
A software project has 80 tasks. If 60 tasks are completed, what percentage of the project is finished?
A data transfer was expected at 100 MB/s but achieved only 80 MB/s. What percentage of the expected speed was achieved?
A laptop battery capacity degraded from 100 Wh to 90 Wh in a year. What is the percentage degradation?
A /24 subnet provides 256 IP addresses. If 64 addresses are currently assigned, what percentage of addresses are free?
Cloud storage costs increased from $0.02/GB to $0.025/GB. What is the percentage increase in cost?
A development team found 40 bugs. If they fixed 32 of them, what percentage of bugs were fixed?
A data center consumes 500 kW of power. If 30% of this power is used for cooling, how much power in kW is used for cooling?
Out of 10,000 users, 7,500 updated to the new software version. What percentage of users updated?
Network latency was reduced from 80ms to 60ms. What is the percentage reduction?
A new processor offers a 20% speed boost over the old one, which ran at 2.5 GHz. What is the new processor's speed in GHz?
A backup operation increased total data size by 10%. If original data was 2 TB, what is the new total size in TB including backup?
A server was down for 2 hours in a 24-hour period. What was its uptime percentage?
A Docker host has 16 GB RAM. Container A uses 25% of the total RAM. How much RAM in GB does Container A use?
Out of 50 attempted cyberattacks, 3 were successful. What is the success rate percentage?
A code file has 200 lines, and 40 of them are comments. What percentage of the lines are comments?

Ratio and Proportion.text: If 3 servers handle 900 requests per second, how many requests can 5 servers handle per second?
A 200KB file is compressed to 40KB. What is the compression ratio (original:compressed)?
A module with 1500 lines of code has 3 bugs. How many bugs would be expected in a 5000-line module with the same bug density?
A 100 Mbps network link is shared by three services in the ratio 2:3:5. How much bandwidth does the service with the largest share receive?
If 4 CPUs can process a task in 6 hours, how long will it take 8 identical CPUs to complete the same task?
A database server handles 1200 queries in 30 seconds. How many queries can it handle in 2 minutes?
64GB of RAM is allocated between two virtual machines (VMs) in the ratio 3:5. How much RAM does the VM with the smaller allocation receive?
A system processes 2500 transactions with 5 errors. What is the error rate per 1000 transactions?
An image with a resolution of 800x600 pixels is scaled up. If the new width is 1200 pixels, what is the new height to maintain aspect ratio?
A dataset grows from 2TB to 2.5TB in 3 months. Assuming a constant growth rate, what will its size be after another 6 months?
A load balancer distributes requests to servers A and B in the ratio 3:2. If server A handles 1500 requests, how many requests does server B handle?
Out of 5000 requests, a cache serves 4500 directly. What is the cache hit rate as a ratio of hits to misses?
A development team has 20 members. The ratio of frontend to backend developers is 3:2. How many backend developers are there?
If a data packet travels 1000 km in 5 milliseconds, how far can it travel in 12 milliseconds at the same speed?
A reviewer checks 300 lines of code in 2 hours. How many hours will it take to review 750 lines at the same rate?
A server rack can hold 42U (units) of equipment. If servers, switches, and patch panels occupy space in the ratio 3:2:1, how many units do the servers occupy?
A system has 99.9% uptime. What is the ratio of uptime to downtime in a given period?
A file of 750MB takes 5 minutes to transfer. How long will a 1.5GB file take to transfer at the same speed?
In a system, the ratio of regular users to administrators is 25:1. If there are 75 administrators, how many regular users are there?
5 servers consume 1200 watts. If 3 more identical servers are added, what is the total power consumption?
An API endpoint processes 150 requests in 10 seconds. How many requests can it handle in 1 minute?
A system requires 4GB of RAM for every 50 users. How much RAM is needed for 300 users?
A $120,000 project budget is split for hardware, software, and labor in the ratio 2:3:5. How much is allocated to software?
Program A processes 100 records in 5 seconds. Program B processes 120 records in 4 seconds. What is the ratio of A's speed to B's speed (records/sec)?
A database with 200 million records is sharded across 4 servers in the ratio 1:2:3:4. How many records are on the server with the largest share?
3 servers process 150 data packets/min. How many packets can 5 servers process in the same time?
A file transfers 20MB in 5 seconds. How long will it take to transfer a 100MB file at the same rate?
A compiler processes 500 lines of code in 10 seconds. How many lines can it process in 1 minute?
A database needs 2GB RAM for 100 concurrent users. How much RAM is needed for 350 concurrent users?
If 40GB of data is transferred over a network in 8 hours, what is the data transfer rate in GB/hour?
In a module of 2000 lines of code, 50 errors were found. How many errors would be expected in a 5000-line module?
A server handles 150 requests/sec. An upgrade increases its capacity in the ratio 2:3 (old:new). What is the new request capacity?
A 500KB file is compressed to 100KB. What is the compression ratio (original:compressed)?
If 4 processor cores can complete 24 tasks in an hour, how many tasks can 6 cores complete in the same hour?
Two applications, App A and App B, share 8GB RAM in the ratio 3:5. How much RAM does App B get?
Out of 1000 packets sent, 20 were lost. What is the ratio of lost packets to successfully sent packets?
A team of 3 reviewers found 45 bugs in a module. If 2 more reviewers join, how many bugs might they find in a similar module?
In a RAID array, 2 out of 10 hard drives failed. If the array had 25 drives, how many failures would be proportional?
A script processes 100 data records in 5 minutes. How long will it take to process 300 records?
A software license costs $500 for 5 users. How much would it cost for 12 users at the same per-user rate?
If 3 cloud instances cost $75/hour, what is the cost for 7 instances per hour?
Out of 200 login attempts, 10 failed. What is the ratio of successful attempts to failed attempts?
A data center uses 5000 Watts for 10 server racks. How much power is needed for 16 racks?
A project team has developers and testers in a ratio of 4:1. If there are 20 developers, how many testers are there?
A network link transfers 120GB in 3 minutes. How much data can it transfer in 5 minutes?
A batch job processes 300 records per hour. How many records will it process in 2 hours and 30 minutes?
A server generates 500MB of log files every 2 days. How many days will it take to fill a 10GB storage?
An encryption algorithm processes 120MB in 4 seconds. How much data can it process in 1 minute?
A distributed system uses 6 nodes to complete 90 tasks. If 2 nodes fail, how many tasks can the remaining nodes complete?
Out of 500 beta users, 300 adopted a new feature. What is the ratio of users who adopted to those who did not?

Time and Work.text: 5 servers handle 100 queries in an hour. How many queries can 3 servers handle in 2 hours?
A senior dev writes a module in 6 hours. A junior dev takes 9 hours. How long do they take together?
Link A transfers 100MB in 5 seconds. Link B transfers 100MB in 10 seconds. How long for 200MB if both links are used?
Dev A fixes bugs twice as fast as Dev B. Together, they fix 30 bugs in 5 hours. How many bugs does Dev A fix alone in 5 hours?
A cloud system provisions 10 VMs in 2 hours. After 30 minutes, an error stops the process. How many VMs were provisioned?
Processor X handles 5 tasks/sec. Processor Y handles 7 tasks/sec. How many tasks do they handle together in 1 minute?
Optimizing a function takes Dev A 10 hours. If Dev B is 20% less efficient than Dev A, how long does Dev B take?
Pipeline A processes 10GB in 4 hours. Pipeline B processes 10GB in 6 hours. If Pipeline A runs for 2 hours, then B takes over, how long does B run to finish 10GB?
4 engineers upgrade 20 servers in 5 days. How many days would 5 engineers take to upgrade 25 servers?
A file downloads in 10 minutes using connection A. With connection B, it takes 15 minutes. How long for both together?
DB A replicates 1TB in 6 hours. DB B replicates 1TB in 12 hours. They start together, but DB B fails after 2 hours. How long does A take to finish the rest?
Algorithm X processes a dataset in 10 minutes. Algorithm Y is 50% faster than X. How long does Y take?

Two cloud storage scripts clean up old data. Script A takes 10 hours alone. Script B takes 15 hours alone. Both start together but Script A stops after 3 hours. How much longer does Script B take to finish?
A network router processes 100 packets/sec. A misconfiguration adds 20 packets/sec to the queue. How long does it take to process 2400 packets if the misconfiguration is fixed?
A QA team can test a feature in 8 days. Another team can do it in 12 days. If the first team works for 4 days, then the second team joins, how many more days to finish?
Server A migrates 1TB in 10 hours. Server B migrates 1TB in 15 hours. Working together, how long to migrate 2TB?
Host X creates 3 VMs/hour. Host Y creates 2 VMs/hour. How many VMs can 2 Host X and 3 Host Y machines create in 4 hours?
Senior dev reviews 100 lines of code in 2 hours. A junior dev takes 3 hours for 100 lines. How many lines can they review together in 6 hours?
3 servers complete a data processing job in 10 hours. If one server goes offline after 4 hours, how long will the remaining servers take to finish the job?
A distributed task is 40% complete in 6 hours. At this rate, how many more hours are needed to complete the task?
A storage buffer fills with data at 5GB/min and empties at 3GB/min. If it starts empty, how long to fill 60GB?
Algorithm A processes 20 records/sec. Algorithm B processes 30 records/sec. If they run on a shared dataset of 3000 records, how long until complete?
Dev X finishes a module in 12 days. Dev Y in 15 days. Dev Z in 20 days. How long for all three to finish it together?
A security scan completes 1/2 of the systems in 4 hours. It then speeds up by 100%. How many more hours to scan the remaining systems?
Dev A and B debug a code in 6 hours. Dev A alone takes 10 hours. How long does Dev B alone take?
A server cluster processes 20 requests per second. How many requests does it process in 4 minutes?
Server A processes a batch of data in 10 minutes. Server B processes the same batch in 15 minutes. How long will they take together?
Developer X writes code twice as fast as Developer Y. If Y takes 9 hours to complete a module, how long will X and Y take together?
5 engineers can fix 20 bugs in 4 days. How many bugs can 10 engineers fix in 2 days?
A critical software update takes 10 hours for a server. After 4 hours, a second server (which also takes 10 hours for the update) joins. How many more hours until the update is complete?
A data buffer fills at a rate of 100MB/min. It's emptied by a process at 60MB/min. If the buffer is initially empty, how long until it holds 1200MB?
3 developers can complete a feature in 15 days. How many developers are needed to complete the same feature in 9 days?
Server Cluster A and B together process a big dataset in 6 hours. Cluster A alone takes 10 hours. How long would Cluster B alone take?
Server 1 handles a batch job in 4 hours, Server 2 in 6 hours. If they work on it alternately for one hour each, starting with Server 1, how long until the job is done?
Developer A codes 3 features for every 2 features coded by Developer B in the same time. If they complete 30 features together in 10 days, how many features did B code?
8 cloud instances can process a dataset in 20 minutes. If 2 instances fail after 5 minutes, how much longer will it take the remaining instances to finish?
A testing suite completes 1/3 of tests in 2 hours. At this rate, how many more hours will it take to complete the remaining tests?
A server, a switch, and a router can process a data packet in 2, 3, and 6 milliseconds respectively. How fast can they process it if working in parallel?
Two developers, Alice and Bob, get $300 for completing a task. Alice takes 10 hours alone, Bob takes 15 hours alone. If they divide payment based on work done, how much does Alice get?
A supercomputer performs computations 5 times faster than a standard server. If the supercomputer completes a job in 4 hours, how long would the standard server take?
A data center's storage system can ingest 50TB/day. A backup process exports data at 20TB/day. If the system starts with 100TB and simultaneously ingests and exports, when will it reach 250TB?
Developer P is 50% more efficient than Developer Q. If Q can complete a coding task in 15 hours, how many hours will P take?
4 servers can process 1000 requests in 2 hours. How many requests can 6 servers process in 3 hours?
A DevOps team aims to deploy 20 features in 5 days. After 2 days, they realize only 8 features are deployed. To meet the deadline, how many features per day must they deploy now?
Server Alpha processes 1/5 of a data chunk in 2 hours. Server Beta processes 1/6 of the same chunk in 2.5 hours. How long will they take to process the entire chunk together?
3 programmers and 4 testers can complete a feature set in 9 days. Each programmer works twice as fast as a tester. How many days would 2 programmers and 2 testers take?
6 dedicated servers can process a large query in 12 minutes. How many servers are needed to process the same query in 8 minutes?
A data center upgrade takes 30 days for 4 engineers. After 10 days, 2 engineers leave. How many more days will the remaining engineers take to finish?
A and B together can develop a software module in 20 days. B and C together can do it in 15 days. C and A together can do it in 12 days. How long will A take alone?
A server's memory buffer can be filled in 10 minutes. An application error can empty it in 15 minutes. If both occur, how long until the buffer is full?

Profit and Loss.text: A tech firm bought a high-performance server for $5000. After 3 years, it sold the server for $3500. What was the percentage loss on the server?
A developer bought 100 software licenses for $2000 total. If they sell each license for $25, what is the total profit?
A data center upgraded its storage for $10,000. It then leased the increased capacity for $1200 per month for a year. What was the net profit or loss after one year?
A software project had a budget of $20,000. If the actual costs were $16,000, and the client was billed for a 25% profit margin on actual costs, what was the profit?
An IT service provider sells a server maintenance contract for $800, making a 25% profit on its cost. What was the cost of providing the service?
A reseller buys 1 TB of cloud storage for $100. If they sell it to customers at a 20% profit, what is the selling price per TB?
An old networking switch was bought for $150. Due to obsolescence, it was sold for $90. Calculate the percentage loss.
A developer subscribes to a premium IDE for $30/month. If they bill clients for usage at a 30% markup, what is the billed amount per month?
A company bought 50 SSDs at $80 each. They received a 10% bulk discount on the total purchase. If they later sold all SSDs at $90 each, what was their total profit?
A web development project was quoted for $5000. Due to unforeseen complexities, the actual cost incurred was $6250. What was the percentage loss for the firm?
An IT reseller purchased a server rack for $300. If they want to make a 20% profit on the selling price, what should be their selling price?
A data recovery service costs $400 to perform. If the company aims for a 35% profit margin on this cost, what should be the service fee?
An e-commerce platform charges $50/month for basic service. If the platform's cost to provide this service is $30, how many subscribers are needed to make a $1000 profit in a month?
A software engineer bought a laptop for $1200. After 2 years, they sold it for a 40% loss. What was the selling price of the laptop?
A company bought 1000 meters of network cable for $0.50 per meter. If 20% of the cable was damaged and unsellable, and the rest was sold for $0.75 per meter, what was the total profit or loss?
A vendor bought SSDs when the market price was $150 each. If they sold them for $135 each, what was their percentage loss?
A software vendor sells a perpetual license for $400, making a 60% profit on its development cost. What was the development cost per license?
A data center invested $5000 in power-efficient cooling systems. This reduced monthly electricity costs by $150. How many months will it take to recover the investment and then start making a profit?
A company purchased 20 high-end GPUs for a rendering farm. The list price was $700 each, but they received a 15% discount on the total order. What was the actual cost per GPU?

A developer licenses a custom code library for $150 per use. If the platform takes a 20% commission on each sale, and the developer's cost per license is $30, what is the developer's net profit per license?
An IT firm quoted a server migration project for $12000, which includes a 20% profit margin on their actual costs. What were the actual costs for the project?

A company bought $1000 worth of cloud credits. They used 70% of them and sold the remaining credits to another firm for a 20% loss on their original cost. What was the selling price of the remaining credits?
A tech training company developed a course for $8000. If they aim for a 25% profit margin on this cost by selling 50 student seats, what should be the selling price per seat?
An enterprise software license costs $50,000. If an organization gets a 10% discount for annual payment but also pays a 5% service charge on the discounted price, what is the final cost?
An IT firm bought used data center equipment for $20,000 and spent an additional $1,000 on transportation and setup. If they sold the equipment for $24,000, what was their percentage profit?
A refurbished server was bought for $800 and sold for $1000. What is the profit percentage?
A batch of 50 defective RAM modules, purchased for $500, was sold for scrap at $350. What was the loss percentage?
A software license costs its developer $120 to create. If they want to make a 25% profit, at what price should it be sold?
A server CPU was sold for $280, incurring a 20% loss. What was its original cost price?
A cloud storage plan, originally priced at $50/month, is now offered for $40/month. What is the discount percentage?
A cybersecurity tool is sold for $180 after a 10% discount. What was its marked price?
A software bundle with a marked price of $200 receives two successive discounts of 10% and 5%. What is the final selling price?
A dealer buys 10 refurbished monitors for $150 each. He sells 8 monitors at $200 each and the remaining 2 at $120 each. What is his total profit or loss?
A seller sold two SSDs for $120 each. On one, he gained 20%, and on the other, he lost 20%. What is the overall percentage gain or loss?
A freelance developer calculated his profit as 20% of his selling price for a service contract. What is his profit percentage on the cost price?
A server installation project cost $2500 for hardware, with $500 in labor and configuration overheads. If sold for $3600, what is the profit percentage?
If a database software license is sold for $600, there is a 20% profit. What would be the profit percentage if it were sold for $500?
A tech device is marked at $300. If a 20% profit is desired after allowing a 10% discount, what is the cost price?
A batch of 100 hard drives was bought for $5000. 60 drives were sold at a 20% profit, and the remaining 40 at a 10% loss. What is the overall profit or loss percentage?
A vendor makes 25% profit by selling cloud credits. If the cost price of the credits increases by 10% and the selling price remains constant, what is the new profit percentage?
A software subscription is offered with successive discounts of 20% and 15%. What is the equivalent single discount?
By selling a network switch for $480, a loss of 20% is incurred. To gain 20%, what should be the selling price?
A store marks up refurbished laptops by 40% and then offers a 20% discount. What is the net profit percentage?
A telecom company charges for 1GB of data but provides only 900MB. If they claim to sell at cost price, what is their actual profit percentage?
A development tool is sold at a 10% profit. If its selling price was $20 less, the profit would be 5%. What is the cost price of the tool?
A vendor bought 500 network cables for $2500. He sold 300 cables at a 20% profit. At what price per cable must he sell the remaining cables to make an overall profit of 15%?
A web development project has a profit of 30% on its selling price. If the selling price is $1500, what is the cost price?
A new smartphone is marked up by 50% above its cost price. A customer buys it for $600 after a 20% discount. What was the original cost price?
A graphic card costs $400 and is marked up by 25%. If it is then sold after a 10% discount, what is the profit percentage?
A company sells an old server for $300, incurring a 25% loss. What was the original cost price of that old server?

Number Series.text: A data queue processes tasks with IDs: 10, 15, 20, 25. What is the ID of the next task?
Server resource allocation doubles with each step: 2, 4, 8, 16. What is the next allocation value?
Function call depths in a recursive algorithm are observed as: 1, 1, 2, 3, 5, 8. What is the next expected depth?
The number of data blocks processed by a CPU core increases as: 1, 4, 9, 16, 25. What is the next number of blocks?
Network latency in milliseconds varies as: 3, 5, 9, 15, 23. What is the next expected latency?
Log file sizes in MB are decreasing linearly: 120, 115, 110, 105. What is the next expected log file size?
Bandwidth allocation for a subnet is halved each time: 1024, 512, 256, 128. What is the next allocation?
Temperature of a CPU (in Celsius) shows an alternating pattern: 45, 50, 46, 51, 47. What is the next temperature?
Processing power in TFLOPS follows the series: 1, 3, 7, 15, 31. What is the next value?

Data transfer in MB for sequential packets: 2, 6, 12, 20, 30. What is the next amount?
Server port numbers are logged in an interleaved series: 21, 80, 23, 81, 25, 82. What is the next port number?
Disk sector IDs are decreasing by perfect squares in reverse order: 100, 81, 64, 49. What is the next ID?
Microservice requests per minute increase by a growing factor: 2, 4, 12, 48, 240. What is the next request count?
Job processing times in seconds are: 50, 48, 44, 38, 30. What is the next processing time?
File version numbers increase by: 1.0, 1.3, 1.7, 2.2, 2.8. What is the next version number?

CPU core usage (%) on two different processes: 10, 20, 15, 25, 20, ?
The number of successful API calls per hour: 4, 9, 19, 39, ?
Data storage remaining (GB) after hourly backups: 100, 52, 28, 16, ?
Encryption key lengths (bits) used in sequence: 2, 3, 5, 7, ?
Number of active firewall rules added weekly: 7, 10, 13, 16, ?
Data transfer rates (MB/s) for different channels: 3, 6, 12, 24, ?
Processing time (ms) for a complex algorithm: 3, 5, 9, 17, ?
Number of user accounts created daily: 1, 2, 6, 24, ?
Available memory blocks in a system: 100, 81, 64, 49, ?
Server load average changes throughout the day: 10, 12, 6, 8, 4, ?
Number of network connections handled per second: 1, 2, 4, 7, 12, ?
Disk space used (GB) by a growing database: 2, 5, 10, 17, ?
Error rate per million requests observed over time: 128, 64, 32, 16, ?
Number of data entries processed per batch: 5, 16, 49, 148, ?
System resource allocation (units) for increasing priority tasks: 8, 16, 32, 64, ?
Bandwidth usage (Mbps) for two parallel streams: 2, 10, 4, 20, 8, ?
