Linear Equations.text: A web server processes 15 requests per second. If it runs for 5 minutes, how many requests does it process?
A data transfer protocol achieves 20 MB/s. How long will it take to transfer a 1.2 GB file? (1 GB = 1024 MB)
A sorting algorithm takes 0.5 milliseconds per item to process. How many items can it process in 10 seconds?
A data structure requires 12 bytes per entry. If a program needs to store 500 entries, how much memory (in KB) is required? (1 KB = 1024 bytes)
A network ping averages 40 milliseconds round-trip. How many pings can be sent and received in 2 minutes?
A cloud server costs $0.05 per hour. If a project runs for 730 hours in a month, what is the total monthly server cost?
A database can process 250 transactions per second. How many transactions can it handle in 10 minutes?
A new tech startup gains 120 new users every day. How many users will they have after 30 days, assuming they start with 0 users?
Each user profile consumes approximately 50 MB of storage. How many user profiles can be stored on a 2 TB hard drive? (1 TB = 1024 GB, 1 GB = 1024 MB)
An API has a rate limit of 300 requests per minute. How many requests can a client make in 2 hours?
A software license costs $150. If a company needs to purchase licenses for 25 developers, what is the total cost?
A server has 8 CPU cores, and each core can process 5 distinct tasks concurrently. What is the total number of distinct tasks the server can handle simultaneously?
A data center uses an average of 1.5 TB of bandwidth per day. How much bandwidth (in TB) will it use in a 30-day month?
During testing, 3 bugs were found in 150 lines of code. If the project has 5000 lines, how many bugs are expected linearly?
Each server in a rack consumes 200 watts of power. If there are 15 such servers, what is the total power consumption in kilowatts (kW)?
A script can process 120 data entries per hour. How many hours will it take to process 1800 data entries?
Each virtual machine (VM) requires 4 GB of RAM. If a host server has 64 GB of RAM, how many VMs can it ideally support?
A large software project compiles 30 files per minute. If there are 450 files in the project, how long will the full compilation take in minutes?
A server generates log data at a rate of 50 MB per hour. What is the total size (in GB) of logs generated in 48 hours? (1 GB = 1024 MB)
Each physical server can host 15 Docker containers. How many physical servers are needed to deploy 225 containers?
A development team completes an average of 7 features per two-week sprint. How many features can they complete in 10 sprints?
A load balancer distributes connections across 5 application servers. If each server can handle 200 concurrent connections, what is the total capacity?
Applying a security patch takes 2.5 minutes per server. How long will it take to patch 60 servers?
A data pipeline processes 100 records every 5 seconds. How many records can it process in 3 minutes?
Daily backups consume 20 GB of storage. If backups are kept for 7 days, how much storage (in GB) is allocated for backups?
A server processes 30 requests/second. How many requests does it process in 5 minutes?
A 250MB file downloads at 5MB/second. How long does it take in seconds?
A program uses 20% of 8GB RAM. How much RAM (in GB) is free?
A cluster has 128 cores distributed among servers. If each server has 8 cores, how many servers are there?
A dev team writes 500 lines of code daily. If a project starts with 1000 lines, how many lines are there after 7 days?
A database stores 100,000 records, each 2KB. What is the total storage needed in MB (1MB = 1000KB)?
A network packet has a base latency of 10ms plus 2ms per router hop. If total latency is 30ms, how many router hops did it make?
A junior developer fixes 3 bugs/day, a senior fixes 5 bugs/day. Together they fixed 32 bugs. If the junior worked 4 days, how many days did the senior work?
A server has 99.5% uptime. In a 30-day month, how many hours was it down?
An API processes 50 requests per second. If it has already processed 1200 requests, how many more seconds will it take to reach a total of 2000 requests?
Cloud storage costs a flat $5 plus $0.02 per GB. If the bill is $15, how many GB were used?
Two CPUs work together on a task. CPU A processes 100 ops/sec, CPU B processes 150 ops/sec. If they complete 5000 operations, how long did it take in seconds?
A new app gains 150 users daily. If it started with 500 users, after how many days will it reach 3500 users?
A system records 1 error for every 500 requests. If there were 25 errors, how many requests were processed?
A 2TB backup drive has 750GB used. How much free space (in GB) remains?
A load balancer distributes 1200 concurrent connections evenly among 4 servers. How many connections does each server handle?
A company buys software licenses at $75 each. If the total cost was $3750, how many licenses were purchased?
A single developer takes 10 hours to build a module. How long would it take 4 developers working at the same rate?
A script processes 2000 data entries in batches of 50. How many batches are needed?
A 100 Mbps internet connection is shared by 5 users. If distributed equally, what bandwidth (in Mbps) does each user get?
A server has 64GB RAM. If each container requires 2GB RAM, how many containers can be deployed?
A log file grows by 15MB/hour. If it starts at 100MB, what size (in MB) will it be after 8 hours?
A project has 200 tasks. If 120 tasks are completed, and a team completes 10 tasks/day, how many more days are needed?
A disk performs 1200 I/O operations in 2 minutes. What is its average operations per second?
A data center has twice as many switches as routers. If there are 30 routers, how many total network devices (switches and routers) are there?

Quadratic Equations.text: An algorithm's performance is modeled by x^2 - 9x + 18 = 0, where 'x' is a critical parameter. Find the possible values of 'x'.
The time 't' (in ms) for a data packet to travel through a congested network is given by t^2 + 5t - 14 = 0. What is the valid positive time 't'?
For a new server configuration, the critical parameters 'p1' and 'p2' are roots of x^2 - 10x + 21 = 0. What is the sum of these parameters?
A system's stability is governed by the equation 2x^2 - 5x + 3 = 0. Are the solutions for 'x' real or complex?
A data array has a total of 120 elements. The number of rows is 2 less than the number of columns. Find the number of columns.
The daily cost 'C' (in dollars) of operating a server farm is given by C(n) = n^2 - 12n + 50, where 'n' is the number of active servers. What is the minimum daily cost?
A new system configuration requires two parameters, 4 and -3. Which quadratic equation has these values as its roots?
For a balanced load, the system parameter 'k' must result in equal roots for x^2 + 6x + k = 0. What is the value of 'k'?
In a data compression algorithm, the loss factors are roots of 3x^2 - 7x + 4 = 0. What is the product of these roots?
A server allocates bandwidth 'B' such that twice its square decreased by five times its value is 12. Find the positive bandwidth 'B'.
If one root of the equation x^2 - kx + 18 = 0 (representing system configuration) is 3, what is the value of 'k'?
A task scheduler balances loads using x + 6/x = 5. What are the possible values for 'x'?
An error propagation model is given by x^2 - 3x + 5 = 0. Do the solutions represent real or complex error values?
If one root of x^2 - 8x + 15 = 0 (representing redundant system parameters) is 3, what is the other root?
For what value of 'c' will the roots of 4x^2 - 17x + c = 0 (representing inverted data mapping) be reciprocals of each other?
In a signal processing task, sqrt(x+5) = x-1. What is the value of 'x'?
A data packet travels a distance 'd' at a rate such that d^2 - 11d + 28 = 0. Find the possible distances 'd'.
A system process can have a parameter 'x' such that x^2 - 5x + c = 0. If one state is x=0, what must 'c' be?
Two performance metrics, 'a' and 'b', are roots of x^2 - 7x + 10 = 0. What is the absolute difference between these metrics?
A server has 'n' CPUs. Doubling the square of 'n' and adding 'n' gives 78. Find 'n'.
If 'r' and 's' are roots of x^2 - 5x + 6 = 0 (representing network flow analysis), what is the value of 1/r + 1/s?
The efficiency 'E' of a data transfer protocol is described by 3E^2 - 10E + 8 = 0. Find the possible efficiency values.
For a hardware component to be compatible, the parameter 'm' must ensure x^2 + 4x + m = 0 has real solutions. What is the maximum integer value of 'm'?
In a network traffic model x^2 + px + 12 = 0, one root is three times the other. Find the positive value of 'p'.
A data block size 'N' satisfies N^2 - 5N = 24. Find the positive block size 'N'.
A server's processing power P is given by P = n^2 + 5n - 14, where n is the number of active processes. If P = 10, what is n?
The data transfer rate R (Mbps) for a network is modeled by R = -t^2 + 10t + 100, where t is time in hours. At what time 't' is the rate 124 Mbps?
The error rate E in a new module depends on the test cases 'x' as E = x^2 - 11x + 30. If E = 6, how many test cases 'x' were run?
A cloud server's uptime 'U' (in hours) after 'd' days is modeled by U = -d^2 + 12d - 20. When is the uptime 15 hours?
The security score S of a system is S = 20 + 8x - x^2, where x is the number of active users (in hundreds). What is the maximum security score?
The cost C ($) of running 'N' instances of a microservice is C = 0.5N^2 - 10N + 80. What number of instances minimizes the cost?
The efficiency E (%) of a sorting algorithm with input size 'n' is given by E = -0.1n^2 + 4n - 10. For what 'n' is the efficiency 20%?
A server's temperature T (in °C) after 'h' hours of continuous operation is T = 0.5h^2 - 3h + 40. When does the temperature reach 40°C?
The latency L (ms) in a data pipeline is L = 2s^2 - 16s + 40, where 's' is the number of processing stages. What is the minimum possible latency?
The number of bugs B in a software release is estimated by B = x^2 - 13x + 42, where x is the number of developers. If B = 0, how many developers could there be?
The download speed S (Mbps) of a file is S = -f^2 + 10f + 20, where 'f' is the file fragmentation percentage. Find 'f' for a speed of 44 Mbps.
The resource utilization U (%) of a server farm is U = -0.5t^2 + 12t - 10, where 't' is the number of active tasks. What number of tasks maximizes utilization?
The network throughput T (Gbps) is T = -L^2 + 8L - 7, where 'L' is the network load factor. If throughput is 8 Gbps, what is L?
The data processing time P (ms) for 'n' units of data is P = 0.5n^2 - 6n + 20. What is the minimum processing time?
The efficiency E of a code block is E = 10 + 4x - x^2, where 'x' is the number of optimization passes. What 'x' gives an efficiency of 13?
The server response time R (ms) is R = t^2 - 12t + 40, where 't' is active connections (in hundreds). What is the minimum response time?
A security algorithm's strength S is S = k^2 - 14k + 50, where 'k' is the key size factor. If S = 5, what is 'k'?
The number of data records processed per second, N, is N = 20x - x^2, where 'x' is the cache size (in MB). What cache size maximizes N?
The power consumption P (watts) of a cooling unit is P = 0.2t^2 - 8t + 100, where 't' is time (hours). What is the minimum power consumption?
A database query speed S (queries/sec) is S = u^2 - 10u + 30, where 'u' is the number of concurrent users. When is the speed 6 queries/sec?
The risk score R of a project is R = m^2 - 16m + 70, where 'm' is the number of project modules. If R = 19, what is 'm'?
The throughput T (requests/sec) of an API gateway is T = -0.5k^2 + 8k + 10, where 'k' is the configuration complexity. What complexity 'k' maximizes throughput?
The cost C ($) of data storage is C = 0.1s^2 - 2s + 15, where 's' is the storage size (TB). When is the cost $7.50?
An algorithm's execution time T (seconds) is T = n^2 - 18n + 90, where 'n' is input size (in hundreds). What input size minimizes the execution time?
The reliability R (%) of a system is R = -0.2f^2 + 4f - 5, where 'f' is the frequency of updates (per month). What frequency 'f' gives a reliability of 10%?

HCF and LCM.text: Server A reboots every 4 hours, Server B every 6 hours. When will they next reboot simultaneously?
A processor has 24 high-priority tasks and 36 low-priority tasks. What is the largest equal number of tasks to process in batches?
Data packets from source X arrive every 8ms, and from source Y every 12ms. When will both packets arrive at the gateway simultaneously again?
A database needs to store 42GB of user data and 56GB of log data. What is the largest common size block it can divide both into?
Service A runs every 3 minutes, Service B every 5 minutes, and Service C every 6 minutes. When will all three services run simultaneously again?
There are 28 frontend developers and 42 backend developers. What is the maximum number of identical teams that can be formed?
A program uses memory blocks of 10KB and 15KB. What is the smallest common memory size that can perfectly fit both block types?
A server has files of 60MB and 90MB. What is the largest possible equal chunk size to transfer both files?
Server maintenance for system X is every 10 days, for system Y every 15 days. When will both systems be maintained on the same day next?
A network router receives data streams of 105 bytes and 140 bytes. What is the largest common packet size it can divide both into?
A system processes data in batches of 7 or 11 items. What is the smallest total number of items it can process perfectly using either batch size?
A programming task has 88 lines of code, and another has 121 lines. What is the largest common number of lines per sub-task if broken down equally?
Three microservices start up in cycles of 2, 4, and 5 seconds respectively. When will all three start at the exact same moment again?
A cluster has 50 CPU cores and 75GB RAM. What is the largest common unit to express available resources for allocation?
System A generates an error log every 12 minutes, System B every 18 minutes. When will they generate an error log at the same time again?
Two log files have sizes of 180MB and 270MB. What is the largest common block size they can be divided into for efficient transfer?
Three database replication processes complete cycles in 6, 8, and 12 seconds. When will all three complete a cycle simultaneously?
A server rack has 40 SSDs and 60 HDDs. What is the maximum number of identical storage configurations that can be assembled?
A web server receives requests from user group X every 9 seconds and user group Y every 15 seconds. When will requests from both groups arrive simultaneously?
A software project has two modules with current versions 72 and 108. What is the largest common version increment they could have shared?
A container platform runs microservices needing 14MB or 21MB RAM. What is the smallest common RAM allocation that can perfectly fit either?
An image has dimensions 1920x1080 pixels. What is the largest square tile size that can perfectly divide both dimensions?
Three data pipelines process data in cycles of 4, 6, and 8 minutes. When will all three pipelines finish a cycle at the same time?
A server has 135 log files and 225 config files. What is the largest number of identical archives that can be created?
A printer can print labels in batches of 20 or 25. What is the minimum number of labels to print that can be exactly divided by both batch sizes?
Two server processes run every 8 minutes and 12 minutes, respectively. If they start together, when will they next run simultaneously?
A system transmits data packets of 60 bytes and 90 bytes. What is the largest common block size these packets can be optimally divided into?
A backup script runs every 6 hours, and a data integrity check runs every 9 hours. If they both ran at 00:00, when will they next run together?
A developer writes code modules with 42 lines and 70 lines. What is the largest common line count for sub-routines they can share?
Three microservices restart every 4, 6, and 8 hours. If they all restart now, when will they all restart simultaneously again?
A database stores user IDs in blocks of 48, 72, and 120. What is the largest common block size for efficient indexing?
The HCF of two server uptime periods is 5 hours, and their product is 1500 hours². What is their LCM?
The LCM of two data transfer rates is 120 Mbps, and their product is 7200 Mbps². What is their HCF?
A developer needs to allocate a minimum number of virtual machines that can be grouped into clusters of either 15 or 25 VMs without remainder.
A memory block of 1024 KB and another of 1280 KB need to be partitioned into the largest possible equal-sized segments, leaving no remainder.
Two network devices ping a server every 10 seconds and 15 seconds. If they ping simultaneously, when is the next simultaneous ping?
A log management system processes events in batches of 144 records and 216 records. What is the largest common sub-batch size for analysis?
A distributed database system needs to replicate data to two nodes. Node A takes 20 minutes, Node B takes 25 minutes. If they start together, when will both complete a full cycle simultaneously?
A cloud provider offers virtual machines with 36 GB and 54 GB storage. What is the largest common partition size that can be used for optimal allocation?
A task scheduler runs job A every 7 days and job B every 11 days. If they both ran today, when is the next day they will both run together?
Two firmware updates are 84 MB and 126 MB. What is the largest common packet size they can be broken into for efficient download?
A script processes data packets. When packets are grouped by 6 or 8, no packets are left over. What is the minimum number of packets processed?
A developer has 95 lines of code and 171 lines of comments. They want to divide both into the largest possible equal-sized blocks, with no leftover lines.
Two network switches send status reports every 30 seconds and 45 seconds. When will their reports next align, given they just reported simultaneously?
A server farm has 210 CPU cores and 315 GB RAM. What is the largest common factor by which these resources can be scaled proportionally?
A data stream arrives in chunks of 18 milliseconds, another in 27 milliseconds. What is the shortest time interval at which their chunk end-points could align?
A security patch is 108 KB, and a system update is 162 KB. What is the largest common file block size for incremental updates?
Two service IDs have an HCF of 7. Their product is 686. What is their LCM?
Two cache expiry times have an LCM of 144 hours. Their product is 1728 hours². What is their HCF?
A distributed queue processes messages every 10 ms, 15 ms, and 20 ms. When is the first time all three queues could finish a cycle together?

Partnership.text: Alice invested $20k and Bob invested $30k in a tech startup. If the annual profit is $10k, how much profit does Bob get?
Clara worked on a module for 6 months, investing 4 hours/day. David worked for 8 months, investing 3 hours/day. If total project profit is $12k, what is Clara's share?
Grace invested $50k in a tech venture. After 6 months, Harry joined with $60k. If the year's profit is $21k, what is Grace's share?
Ivan invested $90k in a data center. After 4 months, he withdrew $30k. Jake invested $70k for the full year. If the total profit is $26k, what is Ivan's share?
Liam and Mia started a software firm with investments of $40k and $50k respectively. Liam receives 10% of the $18k annual profit as salary before distribution. What is Mia's share?
Noah, Olivia, and Peter invested $10k, $12k, and $18k respectively in a new AI startup. If the total profit at year-end is $20k, how much does Peter receive?
A DevOps project earned $15k. Qiana contributed $25k. Her profit share was $6k. How much did Raj invest?
Sam invested $40k for 9 months in a data analytics project. Tina invested $60k. If their profit shares were equal, for how many months did Tina invest?
A tech firm had $25k profit. 20% was allocated to an open-source fund. The rest was shared by Uma and Victor in a 3:2 ratio. What was Uma's share?
Wendy provided $100k capital for a new app development. Xavier contributed intellectual property valued at $80k. If they earn $36k profit, what is Xavier's share?
A server upgrade project cost $150k. After a year, it generated a 10% profit. If Yvonne invested $90k and Zoe invested the rest, what is Zoe's profit?
Alex joined a coding project with $20k. 3 months later, Ben joined with $30k. At the end of 9 months, they generated $10k profit. What is Alex's share?
Carol and Dave invested in a cloud service. Carol's share of profit was $2k more than Dave's. If their investments were $50k and $40k respectively, what was the total profit?
Emily started a tech blog with $10k. 4 months later, Frank joined with $15k. 2 months after Frank, Gary joined with $20k. If the annual profit is $18k, what is Gary's share?
Helen and Ian invested $60k and $40k in a software venture. Helen gets 25% of the $24k profit for managing, with the rest distributed proportionally. What is Ian's share?
Julia invested $75k in a new security algorithm. Her profit share was $9k from a total profit of $24k. What was the total investment for the project?
Kevin invested $30k for 10 months in a blockchain project. Lara invested $50k. If they had equal profit shares, for how many months did Lara invest?
Mike, Nora, and Oscar invested $40k, $50k, and $60k respectively in a server farm. Nora left after 8 months. If the year-end profit was $30k, what is Nora's share?
A software company made $30k profit. $5k was set aside for server maintenance. The remaining profit was shared by Paul and Quinn in a 2:3 ratio. What is Paul's share?
Raj invested $20k for 12 months. Sara invested $30k for 8 months. Tom invested $40k for 6 months. If total profit is $45k, what is Tom's share?
Uma, Vic, and Will invested in a data venture. Uma invested $20k, Vic invested $30k. If the total profit was $27k and Will's share was $12k, how much did Will invest?
Alice invested $30k in a software project. After 4 months, she increased her investment by $10k. Bob invested $50k for the entire year. If the year's profit is $26k, what is Alice's share?

Adam and Bryan are partners in a cyber security firm. Adam receives 5% of the total $40k profit as commission for sales. The remaining profit is split based on their $60k and $40k investments. What is Bryan's share?
Carol invested $70k for 6 months in a network optimization project. David invested $42k. If they split the profit equally, for how many months did David invest?
Emily, Fred, and Gina launched a SaaS startup. Emily invested $50k. Fred invested $70k. If the total profit was $36k and Emily's share was $10k, what was Gina's investment?
Alice contributes 40 hours to a project, Bob contributes 60 hours. If the project earns $2500, how much more does Bob receive than Alice?
Server A runs for 7 months, Server B for 5 months. Both contribute equally to an initial setup cost. If the total revenue is $12000, what is Server A's share?
Three co-founders, C1, C2, and C3, invest $20k, $30k, and $50k respectively in a startup. If the annual profit is $100k, what is C2's share?
AWS, Azure, and GCP provide 20, 30, and 50 VMs for a shared project. If the project's profit is $5000, what is the profit share for GCP?
Drive X invests $4000 for 12 months. Drive Y invests $6000 for 8 months. If the total shared data storage cost is $1000, how much does Drive Y pay?
Dev A works for 6 months, Dev B for 9 months. They split a $1500 bonus pool proportionally to their time. How much does Dev A get?
Rack P invests $1000 for a year. Rack Q invests $1500 for 8 months. The shared annual profit is $3600. What is Rack P's profit share?
Dev A invests $5000, Dev B $7000. The Project Manager takes 20% of the $12000 total project profit as a fee. How much does Dev A get from the remaining profit?
Company X buys a license for $2000. Company Y joins 3 months later, contributing $3000. After a year, they split $8500 profit. How much does Company X get?
ISP A provides 60Mbps, ISP B provides 90Mbps. They share a $3000 monthly profit. How much does ISP B receive?
Dev A invests $2000 for 6 months, then $3000 for the next 6 months. Dev B invests $4000 for the full year. If profit is $6500, what is Dev B's share?
Processor A has 8 cores, Processor B has 12 cores. They process data for a joint project earning $2000. How much does Processor A's owner receive?
Two teams, Alpha and Beta, share a $1200 annual software subscription. Alpha uses 3/5 of the features, Beta uses 2/5. How much does Alpha pay?
SysAdmin P works 2 days a week, SysAdmin Q works 3 days a week. They share a $500 weekly bonus. How much more does Q get than P?
Dev A contributed 4 modules, Dev B contributed 5 modules, Dev C contributed 3 modules to a project. If total profit is $2400, what is Dev C's share?
Team X contributes $500 for 10 months. Team Y contributes $800 for 5 months. If the shared fund yields $900, what is Team X's share?
Server A starts with 1 unit of processing power. Server B joins after 4 months with 2 units. After a year, they share a $1400 profit. What is Server B's share?
John pays $300 for 9 months for a premium code repository access. Mark joins 3 months later, paying $400 for 6 months. They receive an access refund of $850. How much does John get?
Dev X contributes for 5 months, Dev Y for 7 months, and Dev Z for 8 months. They all started with equal initial investment. If the total profit is $4000, what is Dev Y's share?
Server A and Server B each contribute $1000 to an upgrade. Server A is decommissioned after 6 months. Server B continues for 12 months. If the project earns $2400, what is Server B's share?
Developer P fixes 15 bugs, Q fixes 25 bugs. They share a $800 bug bounty. How much does Q receive?
Alice invests $2000 in a software license for a year. Bob joins after 3 months with $3000, and after 6 more months, increases his investment by $1000. If total profit is $18000, what is Bob's share?
Two firms co-locate servers. Firm A uses 3 units for 8 months. Firm B uses 4 units for 6 months. They share a $3500 maintenance bill. How much does Firm A pay?
Dev X contributes 15 lines of code per day for 20 days. Dev Y contributes 20 lines per day for 15 days. If the project receives a $3000 grant, how much does Dev X get?

Founder A invests $50k for 10 months. Founder B invests $60k for 8 months. A third investor, C, gets $2000 as a fixed bonus before profit distribution. If the total profit is $11800, what is Founder A's share from the remaining profit?

Simple Interest.text: A tech startup secured a $12,000 seed funding. If the simple annual return rate is 5%, what is the interest earned after 4 years?
A server upgrade costs $8,000. If the financing has a simple interest rate of 10% per annum, what is the total amount due after 2.5 years?
A data storage solution generated $1,500 in simple interest over 5 years. If the annual interest rate was 6%, what was the initial investment?
A software development project funded with $10,000 returned $2,500 in simple interest after 5 years. What was the annual interest rate?
An IT department invested $20,000 and earned $3,000 in simple interest at an annual rate of 6%. For how many years was the investment held?
A cloud server bill accumulated to $13,000 over 4 years, including a 15% simple annual interest on the initial cost. What was the original cost?
A software license costing $5,000 accumulated to $6,000 after 4 years due to simple annual interest. What was the interest rate?
A new hardware purchase of $7,500 grew to a total cost of $9,000 at a simple annual interest rate of 8%. How long did this take?
An investment in server infrastructure doubles its value in 8 years at a simple interest rate. What is the annual interest rate?
How many years will it take for a tech grant to triple its value at a simple annual interest rate of 10%?
A developer buys a gadget for $600 with a simple interest loan of 12% per annum. What is the interest for 6 months?
An urgent data recovery service cost $2,000. If financed at 8% simple annual interest for 9 months, what's the total repayment?
A freelancer borrowed $4,000 for a new workstation and paid $150 in simple interest over 3 months. What was the annual interest rate?
After 18 months, a project generated $900 in simple interest at an annual rate of 10%. What was the initial project investment?
An expanded server rack cost $10,500 after 2 years due to a 25% simple annual interest rate on its initial price. What was the principal cost?
A tech scholarship of $1,500 grew to $1,800 in 2 years due to simple annual interest. What was the interest rate?
A cybersecurity project with an initial budget of $4,000 reached a total expenditure of $4,800 at a simple annual interest rate of 5%. How long did it take?
A software license costs $500. If bought on credit with a simple annual interest rate of 10% for 2 years, how much extra is paid compared to cash purchase?
A tech investment generated $400 simple interest in 2 years and $800 in 4 years. What was the initial investment if the annual rate was 10%?
At what simple annual interest rate will $5,000 become $6,250 in 5 years?
In what time will $3,000 generate $450 simple interest at an annual rate of 6%?
A developer invested $5,000 at 8% simple interest for 3 years, and another $4,000 at 10% for 2 years. What is the total simple interest earned?
If the simple interest on a tech investment for 3 years increases by $150 when the annual rate rises from 5% to 10%, what is the principal?
A blockchain project received $7,500 initial funding. If it yields 4% simple annual interest, how much interest is earned after 1 year and 6 months?
A loan for office tech equipment doubles in 12.5 years at a certain simple interest rate. In how many years will it triple at the same rate?
A startup invests $5000 in a new server rack. If it earns a simple annual return of 8% for 3 years, what is the total interest earned?
A developer loan of $2000 is taken at 5% simple annual interest. What is the total amount due after 4 years?
An initial data storage of 10TB grows at a simple interest rate of 10% annually. How much extra data storage is needed after 5 years?
A tech company borrowed $15,000 for 2 years at a simple interest rate. If they paid $1800 in total interest, what was the annual interest rate?
A new software license costs $800. After 3 years, its value depreciated by a simple interest model to $680. What was the annual depreciation rate?
A developer buys a new high-end PC for $2500. If its resale value depreciates at a simple rate of 12% per year, what is its value after 2.5 years?
A server upgrade project received $10,000 in funding. If it yields a simple annual profit of 7.5% for 4 years, what is the total profit?
An initial code repository of 5000 lines of code grows by 2% simple interest monthly. How many new lines of code are added in 6 months?
A data center pays $3600 in total simple interest on a loan of $20,000 over 3 years. What is the annual interest rate?
An initial memory allocation of 16GB is increased by a simple rate of 5% quarterly. What is the total memory after 2 quarters?
A cloud service initial annual cost is $1200. If a simple interest penalty of 3% per year is applied to this initial cost for 5 years, what is the total penalty amount?
A programmer lent $1000 to a colleague. If the colleague repays $1120 after 2 years with simple interest, what was the annual interest rate?
A company invests $20,000 in R&D and expects a simple return. If they receive $24,000 after 4 years, what was the annual return rate?
What principal amount needs to be invested at 4% simple annual interest to earn $480 in 3 years?
A new networking device costs $1500. It depreciates by 10% simple annual interest. What is its value after 18 months?
How long will it take for $3000 to grow to $3600 at a simple annual interest rate of 5%?
A cybersecurity firm estimates $12,000 in potential losses from a vulnerability. If a fix reduces this by 2.5% simple annual value for 6 years, what is the total value saved?
What is the simple annual interest rate if $8000 amounts to $9200 in 5 years?
A startup needs $7000 in 5 years for a project. If they can invest at a simple annual interest rate of 8%, how much should they invest now?
An initial batch of 200 security patches yields a simple daily improvement of 0.5%. How many patches will be improved in 10 days?
A project's initial budget of $50,000 increased by $6000 in 2 years due to simple interest on delays. What was the annual interest rate of the delay cost?
How much time is required for $12,000 to earn $1800 simple interest at an annual rate of 7.5%?
A startup purchased a cloud server for $3500. If it depreciates by a simple annual rate of 8%, what is its depreciated value after 9 months?
If a system's processing speed increased from 1000 ops/sec to 1300 ops/sec in 3 years with simple growth, what was the annual growth rate?
What amount will a tech grant of $6000 yield after 2 years at a simple annual interest rate of 6.5%?

Compound Interest.text: A tech startup invests $10,000 of initial capital at an annual interest rate of 5% compounded annually. What will be the total amount after 2 years?
A server farm's maintenance budget of $20,000 earns interest at 4% compounded semi-annually. What is the amount after 1 year?
A software engineer invests $5,000 into a high-yield tech fund paying 8% interest compounded quarterly. How much interest is earned in 6 months?
An e-commerce platform needs $12,100 in 2 years for a server upgrade. If they can invest at 10% compounded annually, how much should they invest now?
A data scientist saves $1,000 in an account offering 6% interest compounded monthly. What will be the amount after 3 months?
A venture capitalist invests $50,000 into a promising AI startup at 7% compounded annually for 3 years. What is the final amount?
A gaming company invested $20,000 and it grew to $24,200 in 2 years, compounded annually. What was the annual interest rate?
An IT department has $3,650 in an emergency fund earning 5% compounded daily. What is the amount after 1 day? (Assume 365 days/year)
A developer invested $8,000 at 10% compounded annually. How many years will it take for the investment to reach $9,680?
A startup's seed funding is projected to grow at 12% compounded annually. Approximately how long will it take for the funding to double?
An investor considers two accounts: Account A offers 6% compounded annually, Account B offers 5.9% compounded semi-annually. Which has a higher effective annual rate?
To have $10,404 for a server upgrade in 1 year, with an investment earning 4% compounded semi-annually, how much must be invested now?
A software product's recurring revenue of $100,000 is invested at 8% compounded annually. What is the interest earned in the second year?
A company's innovation fund starts with $25,000, growing at 6% compounded quarterly for 2 years. What is the final value?
An initial investment of $10,000 in a new tech gadget yields $11,025 in 1 year, compounded semi-annually. What is the annual interest rate?
A dev team's bonus of $2,000 is invested for 1 year at 12% compounded monthly. What is the total amount?
A cloud service offers a discount structure of 8% compounded quarterly. What is the effective annual discount rate?
A network engineer wants to earn $3,000 interest in 2 years by investing in a 5% annually compounded fund. How much must they initially invest?
A cybersecurity firm's profit of $15,000 is invested at 4% compounded annually for 3 years. What is the total interest earned?
A data center invests $40,000 at 6% interest compounded semi-annually for 1 year. What is the total interest earned?
A software development budget of X needs to grow to $10,150.75 in 3 months. If it earns 6% compounded quarterly, what is X?
An initial $25,000 investment for cloud services grows to $31,492.80. If the annual compound rate is 8%, how many years did it take?
A developer borrowed $5,000, which amounted to $5,151.01 after 6 months, compounded monthly. What was the annual interest rate?
An IT company receives $10,000. Option A: 5% simple interest for 2 years. Option B: 4.8% compounded annually for 2 years. Which yields more?
A software license fund starts with $7,500 at 6% interest compounded annually. What is the interest earned in the 4th year?
A startup invests $10,000 in cloud servers. If the value appreciates by 5% annually, compounded annually, what is its value after 2 years?
A legacy database system costs $5,000 to maintain annually. If maintenance costs increase by 2% per year, compounded quarterly, what will be the cost after 1 year?
An initial investment of $8,000 is made for data center upgrades. If it grows at 6% annually, compounded semi-annually, what is the value after 1 year?
A tech company invests $20,000 in a secure fund earning 4% interest, compounded annually. How much interest is earned after 3 years?
A software license costs $1,200 initially. If its renewal price increases by 1% per month, compounded monthly, what is the cost after 6 months?
A startup's initial capital of $50,000 grows at an annual rate of 7%, compounded annually. What will be the capital after 5 years?
A server rack purchased for $3,000 appreciates in value (due to specialized components) by 3% per year, compounded annually. What is its value after 4 years?
A cloud service subscription costs $2,400 annually. If its price increases by 8% per year, compounded quarterly, what will be the annual cost after 1 year?
An IT department invests $15,000 in equipment. If it yields 5% annually, compounded semi-annually, what is the total amount after 2 years?
A project's initial budget for data storage is $1,000. If this budget is expected to increase by 6% annually, compounded annually, what will be the increase in budget after 2 years?
A software development team sets aside $5,000. If this fund earns 0.5% interest per month, compounded monthly, what will it be worth in 1 year?
A company budgets $25,000 for a network upgrade. If the cost of upgrades increases by 4% per year, compounded annually, what is the expected cost after 3 years?
An initial investment of $12,000 is made in cybersecurity infrastructure. If it accrues value at 3% annually, compounded semi-annually, what is the value after 2 years?
An API usage fee starts at $500 per quarter. If the fee increases by 1% per quarter, compounded quarterly, what will be the fee in 1 year?
A research and development grant of $40,000 is awarded. If it accumulates at 3.5% per annum, compounded annually, what is the total amount after 4 years?
The cost of training an AI model is $800. If this cost increases by 0.8% monthly, compounded monthly, what is the cost after 3 months?
A company invests $30,000 in blockchain technology projects. If it earns 7% interest, compounded annually, how much interest is earned after 2 years?
An IT department plans a $10,000 upgrade. If they save for it at 4% annually, compounded semi-annually, how much will they have after 1.5 years?
A quantum computing research fund of $60,000 grows at an annual rate of 5%, compounded quarterly. What will be the fund's value after 1.5 years?
An initial budget of $7,500 is allocated for a software project. If the budget increases by 2.5% annually, compounded annually, what is the budget after 2 years?
The initial cost of a developer training program is $1,500. If the cost increases by 0.7% monthly, compounded monthly, what is the cost after 5 months?
A company invests $18,000 in a big data storage solution. If it accrues value at 6% annually, compounded annually, how much interest is generated after 3 years?
The initial cost to implement a microservices architecture is $22,000. If costs increase by 4% annually, compounded semi-annually, what is the total cost after 2 years?
A fund of $35,000 is set aside for tech acquisitions. If it earns 6% annually, compounded quarterly, what is the amount after 1 year?
An IT asset is valued at $9,000. If its value increases by 5% annually, compounded annually, what will be its value after 3 years?

Progressions (AP/GP).text: A server takes 10s for the first boot, and each subsequent boot is 2s faster. What's the total time for 5 boots?
A database initially stores 100GB. If its storage requirement doubles every year, how much will it store in the 4th year?
A developer fixes 5 bugs on the first day, and 2 more bugs each subsequent day. How many bugs are fixed on the 7th day?
A network stream sends 2 packets in the first second. Each second, it sends three times the number of packets as the previous second. How many total packets are sent in 4 seconds?
A server rack initially holds 8 units. Each new rack added holds 3 more units than the previous one. How many units does the 5th rack hold?
A data transfer process sends 100MB in the first minute. Each subsequent minute, it sends 90% of the previous minute's amount. What is the total data transferred if this continues indefinitely?
A software project initially takes 10 weeks. If the 3rd similar project takes 16 weeks and project completion times form an AP, how long will the 7th project take?
A processor's speed is 2GHz. If its speed increases by 20% every generation, what will be the speed of the 4th generation processor?
A cluster of 10 servers has daily uptimes forming an AP. The minimum uptime is 90 hours and the maximum is 108 hours. What is the total uptime for all 10 servers?
A recursive function processes data where the size is halved at each step. If the initial data size is 128 units, how many steps are needed until the data size is 1 unit?
A server's log file starts at 50MB. After 4 days, it's 86MB. Assuming a constant daily increase, what is the daily increase in MB?
A malware infects 2 machines initially. After 3 hours, 54 machines are infected. If the infection spreads geometrically, what is the hourly multiplication factor?
To optimize a process, 3 new intermediate steps are added between an initial step (10ms) and a final step (30ms). If all 5 steps form an AP, what is the time for the second step?
The cost of a certain cloud service tier is $100 per month. If the cost increases by 5% each year, what will be the monthly cost in the 4th year?
A team spends 30 minutes on server maintenance on Monday. Each day they spend 5 minutes more than the previous day. How much total time do they spend from Monday to Friday?

A data recovery tool recovers 200 files in the first pass. Each subsequent pass recovers half the files of the previous one. If the process stops when fewer than 5 files are recovered, how many passes are made?
A CPU's temperature increases by 2°C each hour after 1 PM. If it was 30°C at 1 PM, what is the sum of temperatures at 2 PM, 3 PM, and 4 PM?
A new application gains users such that in the first month there are 100 users. Each subsequent month, the user base triples. How many total unique users has the app had by the end of 3 months?
A data analyst completes 15 tasks on the first day. Each subsequent day, they complete 2 more tasks than the previous day. If they completed a total of 120 tasks, for how many days did they work?
A program allocates 2KB of memory initially. In each of the next 4 steps, it allocates double the memory of the previous step. What is the total memory allocated after 5 steps?
A monitoring system reports 15 critical alerts on day 1 and 25 critical alerts on day 5. If alerts increase by a constant amount daily, what is the total number of alerts in the first 5 days?

In a distributed system, a task is processed by 3 machines in the final stage. Each preceding stage involved twice as many machines as the subsequent stage. If there are 4 stages in total, how many machines were involved in the first stage?
The average processing time for 7 batches of data forms an AP. If the first batch takes 120ms and the 7th batch takes 180ms, what is the average processing time for all 7 batches?
A codebase has 50 lines. After each of 3 optimization rounds, the line count increases by 20% relative to the previous round. What is the line count after the 3rd round?
A bug tracking system reports 10 new bugs on Monday, 13 on Tuesday, and so on, following an AP. On which day (day number) will 31 new bugs be reported?
A server log starts at 100MB and grows by 15MB each day. What will be its size on the 8th day?
A data packet is retransmitted. Each retransmission attempt takes twice as long as the previous. If the first takes 5ms, how long will the 5th retransmission attempt take?
A developer commits 5 lines of code on day 1, 8 lines on day 2, 11 lines on day 3, and so on. How many lines of code are committed in the first 10 days?
A server virus infects 2 machines initially. Each infected machine then infects 3 new machines in the next hour. How many total machines are infected after 4 hours (including initial)?
A data center starts with 500TB storage and adds 75TB monthly. In how many months will it reach 1100TB storage?
An algorithm's processing speed doubles with each optimization iteration. If it starts at 1 unit/sec, how many iterations until it reaches 64 units/sec?
5 servers handle a total of 1500 requests/minute. The requests assigned form an AP, and the smallest server handles 200 requests. What is the largest server's load?
A data transfer system sends 100MB in the first second, then 50MB in the second, 25MB in the third, and so on. What is the total data transferred if it continues indefinitely?
Three servers have CPU loads in an AP. The first server has 40% load, and the third has 80% load. What is the CPU load of the second server?
Three network nodes introduce latency in a GP. The first node adds 2ms, and the third adds 8ms. What latency does the second node add?
A program allocates memory blocks in an AP: 10KB, 14KB, 18KB... Which block number will be 50KB?
A data compression algorithm reduces file size by half in each pass. If an initial file is 128MB, after how many passes will it be 4MB?
The monthly uptime of a server was 99.5%, 99.6%, 99.7% for the first three months. Assuming an AP, what is the common difference?
A network's available bandwidth reduces by a constant factor each hour: 100Mbps, 50Mbps, 25Mbps. What is the common ratio of this GP?
A series of 7 daily backups are stored. The first backup is 10GB and the last is 40GB, forming an AP. What is the total size of all backups?
A refactoring project saves 10 hours in the first week. Each subsequent week, savings are 1.5 times the previous week. What are the total hours saved in the first 3 weeks?
Three servers consume power in an AP, totaling 1800W. The smallest consumes 500W. What is the power consumption of the largest server?
A data transfer experiences latencies of 4ms for the 2nd hop and 36ms for the 4th hop, forming a GP. What is the common ratio?
Which sequence of server loads represents an Arithmetic Progression?
Which sequence of data processing speeds represents a Geometric Progression?
A database processes 100 queries in the first hour. Each subsequent hour, it processes 20 more queries than the previous. How many total queries are processed in 5 hours?
A recursive function calls itself 3 times on the first level. Each subsequent level makes 2 times the calls of the previous level. How many total calls are made across 4 levels?
Three server racks have temperatures in AP, totaling 75°C. The common difference is 5°C. What is the temperature of the middle rack?
A data transfer rate increases in an AP. The 3rd minute shows 20MB/s and the 7th minute shows 44MB/s. What was the rate in the 1st minute?
A complete binary tree has 7 nodes in total. If each level has twice the nodes of the previous, and it has 3 levels, how many nodes are on the first level?

Logarithms.text: If a server's storage capacity grows to 2^x GB, and log₂(2^x) = 10, what is the new capacity?
A network's latency factor is log(A) + log(B). Express this as a single logarithm for total latency.
The data compression ratio is log₂(X) - log₂(Y). Simplify this expression.
An algorithm's steps are described by 3 log₂(N). Simplify this expression using logarithm properties.
Convert log₅(25) to an expression using base 10 logarithms.
In a system where data traffic starts at '1' unit, what is log₁₀(1) representing the initial traffic log?
If an event has a probability factor of log₂(2), what is its numerical value?
A decrypted message uses 2^(log₂(X)). What is the original message?
If log₂(Capacity) = 8, what is the required capacity in GB?
A system grows such that log_b(125) = 3. What is the growth base 'b'?
How many bits are needed to uniquely identify 1024 items, represented by log₂(1024)?
A server's processing power is log₅(5⁸). What is its simplified power value?
Express log₁₀(1/1000) representing signal attenuation.
The combined performance index is log₂(8) + log₂(4). What is the total index?
A processing speed difference is log₃(81) - log₃(3). Calculate this difference.
A database query's complexity is 2 log₅(25). Evaluate this complexity.
For continuous growth modeled by e, what is the value of ln(e⁵)?
Which integer is closest to log₁₀(900)?
Simplify the expression: log₂(16x²) - log₂(x).
What is the maximum depth of a balanced binary search tree with 256 nodes? (log₂(N))
If an event has probability P, its information content is -log₂(P). For P = 1/32, what is the information content?
Which of these logarithmic expressions is the smallest?
A server's performance decreases such that P(t) = P₀ * (1/2)^(t/5). If performance is 1/8 of P₀, how many 5-year cycles (t/5) passed?
A data center noise level increases by a factor of 100. If the initial noise was log₁₀(X), what is the new noise expressed logarithmically?
Evaluate the natural logarithm expression: 2ln(e) + ln(1).
An algorithm's complexity is O(log₂ N). For N=1024 data items, how many operations are indicated?
A server's performance gain is `log₂(C)` where C is CPU cores. If C=16, what is the performance gain factor?
The memory usage of a program at time `t` is `ln(t)`. If the current usage is 6 units, at what `t` was this measured?
A network signal strength drops from 100mW to 1mW. Calculate the decibel loss using `10 * log₁₀(P_final / P_initial)`.
For a sorted array of 128 elements, what is the maximum number of comparisons a binary search performs?
The average probe count in a hash table is `log₂(N)`. For a table with N=512 entries, what is the average probe count?
A compression algorithm yields a ratio `log₂(Original / Compressed)`. If original is 1GB and compressed is 128MB, what is the ratio?
The number of bits needed to represent `K` unique security keys is `log₂(K)`. How many bits for `K = 65536` keys?
A new routing protocol reduces latency by a factor `log₃(81)`. What is this factor?
A cloud resource allocator uses `log₁₀(Demand)` units. If demand is `100,000` VMs, how many resource units are allocated?
If a system can handle `2^V` simultaneous requests, and `V` is given by `log₂(1024)`, what is `V`?
An operation's complexity is `log₂(N^4)`. Simplify this logarithmic expression.
A B-tree's height is `log_k(N)`. If k=8 (branch factor) and N=4096 (data records), what is the tree's height?
The error magnitude in data transmission is `ln(1/P_error)`. If `P_error = e^(-5)`, what is the error magnitude?
A CPU's benchmark score `S` is related to clock cycles `C` by `S = log₂(C)`. If `C = 256` cycles, what is the score `S`?
A load balancer routes requests using `log_b(R)`. If `log_3(R) = 4`, how many requests `R` are being handled?
Debugging time for N lines of code is `log₂(N)`. How many times longer for 256 lines than for 16 lines?
Password strength `S` in bits is `log₂(P)`, where `P` is the number of possibilities. If `P = 2^64`, what is `S`?
The number of nodes `N` in a specific network topology relates to its depth `d` by `N = 2^d`. If `N = 1024`, what is `d`?
Data center power consumption `P` grows as `ln(S)`, where `S` is the number of servers. If `S = e^7`, what is `P`?
An algorithm's efficiency gain is `log₅(625)`. What is this gain factor?
Disk storage density `D` can be represented by `log₂(C)` bits per block, where `C` is the block capacity. If `C=32`, how many bits is `D`?
A quantum algorithm optimizes problem size by `log₂(N^3)`. Simplify this logarithmic expression.
The API call rate limit `R` is `log₁₀(X)` calls per second. If `X = 1,000,000`, what is the rate limit `R`?
Cache misses `M` decrease logarithmically with cache size `S`. If `log₄(S) = 5`, what is the effective cache size `S`?

Averages.text: A load balancer directs requests to three servers with response times of 100ms, 120ms, and 80ms. What is the average response time?
Five users collectively downloaded 2GB of data from a server. What is the average data downloaded per user?
A server's CPU utilization was recorded as 60%, 80%, 70%, and 90% over four consecutive hours. What was the average CPU utilization?
Four developers contributed 500, 600, 400, and 700 lines of code to a module. What is the average lines of code per developer?
A network router has three interfaces handling 1000, 1200, and 800 packets per second, respectively. What is the average packet rate per interface?
A database executed five queries with completion times of 20ms, 25ms, 15ms, 30ms, and 20ms. Calculate the average query execution time.
A folder contains four log files with sizes 10MB, 15MB, 8MB, and 17MB. What is the average size of a log file?
During three testing cycles, a software project had 12, 18, and 9 bugs reported. What is the average number of bugs per cycle?
Two servers in a cluster reported an uptime of 99.8% and 99.6% last month. What is their average uptime?
A web application recorded daily unique users as 100, 110, 120, 130, 140, 150, and 160 over a week. What is the average daily unique user count?
Three virtual machines (VMs) are using 200GB, 250GB, and 150GB of disk space, respectively. What is the average storage consumed per VM?
Four consecutive code builds took 30s, 40s, 25s, and 35s to complete. What is the average compilation time?
In a week, five developers made 10, 15, 8, 12, and 20 commits to a repository. What is the average number of commits per developer?
Three running processes consumed 50MB, 60MB, and 40MB of RAM, respectively. What is the average memory usage per process?
Two data transfers achieved throughputs of 100Mbps and 120Mbps. What is the average throughput of these transfers?
Four ping tests to a server resulted in latencies of 10ms, 12ms, 8ms, and 14ms. Calculate the average network latency.
Three server components consume 20W, 25W, and 15W of power, respectively. What is their average power consumption?
Over four 5-minute intervals, a database server handled 50, 60, 40, and 70 concurrent connections. What was the average number of concurrent connections?
Three security scans on a system found 6, 9, and 3 vulnerabilities, respectively. What is the average number of vulnerabilities found per scan?
A support team resolved three tech tickets in 2 hours, 3 hours, and 1 hour. What is the average resolution time per ticket?
A cloud storage service processed two data transfers of 500MB and 700MB. What is the average data amount transferred?
Three source code files contain 15, 20, and 10 functions, respectively. What is the average number of functions per file?
The temperatures of four server components were recorded as 60°C, 65°C, 55°C, and 70°C. What is the average component temperature?
A system uses three databases that processed 1000, 1200, and 800 transactions per second during peak load. What is the average transaction rate?
Five web page loads recorded times of 2s, 2.5s, 1.5s, 3s, and 2s. What is the average page load time?
A server processed 1200 requests in 5 minutes. What was the average requests per minute?
A programmer wrote 300 lines of code on Monday, 250 on Tuesday, and 350 on Wednesday. What's the average daily lines of code?
Four data packets had sizes: 100KB, 120KB, 80KB, 140KB. What is their average size?
A data transfer completed 2000 MB in 5 minutes. What was the average transfer rate in MB/minute?
Ping times were 30ms, 45ms, 35ms, and 50ms. What is the average latency?
A server's CPU usage was 50% for 3 hours and 70% for 2 hours. What was the average CPU usage over 5 hours?
In a database, 5 queries took 10ms each and 3 queries took 20ms each. What is the average execution time per query?
A log file contains 15 entries for 'Error', 25 for 'Warning', and 10 for 'Info'. What is the average number of entries per type?
Three virtual machines used 4GB, 6GB, and 8GB RAM respectively. What is the average RAM usage per VM?
A network switch handled 1200 packets in the first hour and 1800 packets in the second hour. What's the average packets per hour?
A programming team completed 2 features in 4 days, 3 features in 6 days, and 1 feature in 2 days. What is the average number of days per feature?
A dataset has values: 10, 20, 30, 40, 50. If 60 is added, what is the new average?
The average download speed for 4 users was 80 Mbps. If a fifth user downloads at 100 Mbps, what's the new average speed?
A cache stores items with sizes: 2KB, 3KB, 5KB, 6KB, 4KB. What is the average item size?
Over 7 days, a server had 3 crashes. What is the average crash rate per day?
A database system processes transactions at an average of 150 transactions/second for 10 minutes. How many total transactions were processed?
A network link had packet loss rates of 1%, 2%, 1.5%, 2.5%, 3%. What is the average packet loss rate?
A system backup took 30 minutes. If the average speed was 20 MB/s, what was the total data backed up (in GB)?
Three developers have committed 50, 70, and X lines of code. If the average is 65 lines, what is X?
A data array contains the numbers 5, 8, 12, 15. If the average of these numbers and 'y' is 10, what is 'y'?
A cloud service costs $120 for 3 months. What is the average monthly cost?
A web application received 600 requests on day 1 and 800 requests on day 2. What is the average daily requests?
A group of 5 servers consumed an average of 200W each. If one server consumed 250W, what was the total power consumption of the other 4?
A software project has 3 modules with 10, 15, and 20 bugs respectively. What is the average number of bugs per module?
The average load on 4 CPUs was 0.75. If three CPUs had loads of 0.7, 0.8, and 0.6, what was the load on the fourth CPU?

Bar Graphs.text: A bar graph illustrates server uptime: January 99.5%, February 99.2%, March 99.8%, April 99.0%. Which month had the highest uptime?
A bar graph displays data transfer rates (MB/s) for four connections: C1 120, C2 90, C3 150, C4 110. What is the average transfer rate?
Bug counts for three software modules are: Module A 45, Module B 60, Module C 35. How many more bugs are in Module B than Module C?
Developer commits last week: Alice 20, Bob 30, Charlie 10, David 40. What percentage of total commits did David make?
Server response times (ms): Server X 150, Server Y 200, Server Z 120, Server W 180. Which server has the fastest response time?
Disk usage (GB) for four partitions: P1 150, P2 200, P3 100, P4 250. What is the total disk usage?
Monthly active users for four applications: AppA 1200, AppB 1500, AppC 900, AppD 1800. What is the difference between the highest and lowest user count?
A survey on language usage shows: Python 40%, Java 30%, C++ 15%, Go 10%, Others 5%. Which language is used by the smallest percentage among the named languages?
Average network latency (ms) to four data centers: DC1 50, DC2 30, DC3 80, DC4 40. Which data center has the highest latency?
CPU core utilization for a process: Core 1 70%, Core 2 60%, Core 3 80%, Core 4 50%. What is the average CPU core utilization?
Memory consumption (MB) by four microservices: MS1 250, MS2 300, MS3 200, MS4 350. How much more memory does MS4 consume than MS3?
Projects completed on time by teams: Team Alpha 8, Team Beta 12, Team Gamma 6, Team Delta 10. What is the total number of projects completed on time?
Data processing speed (records/sec) of four algorithms: Algo1 1000, Algo2 800, Algo3 1200, Algo4 900. Which algorithm processes data the fastest?
Number of deployed containers per environment: Dev 50, Test 80, Staging 30, Prod 120. Which environment has the fewest deployed containers?
Hourly API requests for four endpoints: EP1 5000, EP2 7500, EP3 3000, EP4 6000. What is the combined request volume for EP1 and EP3?
Average query execution times (ms) for four database types: MySQL 25, PostgreSQL 30, MongoDB 15, Redis 10. Which database performs queries the slowest?
Lines of code for new features: Feature A 800, Feature B 1200, Feature C 600, Feature D 1000. What is the total lines of code across these features?
Monthly bandwidth usage (TB) for four regions: North 50, South 30, East 70, West 40. What is the average monthly bandwidth usage?
Number of VM instances running in different clusters: Cluster A 20, Cluster B 30, Cluster C 15, Cluster D 25. If 5 more instances are added to Cluster C, what is its new count?
User adoption for software versions: V1.0 40%, V1.1 35%, V1.2 20%, Other 5%. Which version has the second-highest adoption rate?
Build times (minutes) for four different projects: Project P 15, Project Q 20, Project R 10, Project S 25. Which project has the shortest build time?
Error rates (%) for four microservices: Service A 0.5, Service B 1.2, Service C 0.8, Service D 0.3. Which service has the lowest error rate?
Data stored (TB) across different storage types: Block 100, Object 150, File 80, Archive 200. What is the total storage in Block and File types?
Usage percentage of DevOps tools: Jenkins 40%, Gitlab CI 30%, CircleCI 15%, Azure DevOps 15%. If 100 teams use these, how many use Jenkins?
Monthly system uptime (hours): Server A 720, Server B 715, Server C 718. If a month has 730 hours, how much downtime did Server B experience?
A bar graph shows daily data transfer (in GB) for four network links. Link A: 150 GB, Link B: 200 GB, Link C: 120 GB, Link D: 180 GB. What is the combined data transfer of Link A and Link C?

A bar graph displays bug counts for four software modules: Module X (25 bugs), Module Y (18 bugs), Module Z (32 bugs), Module W (15 bugs). What is the difference between the module with the most and the least bugs?

A bar graph illustrates disk space usage (in GB) for four project folders: Alpha (500 GB), Beta (750 GB), Gamma (300 GB), Delta (650 GB). If the total available disk space is 2.5 TB, what percentage of the total space is used by Project Beta?
A bar graph shows API request latency (in ms) for four quarters: Q1 (120ms), Q2 (150ms), Q3 (100ms), Q4 (130ms). What is the average API request latency over the year?
A bar graph depicts lines of code contributed by four developers in a sprint: Dev A (2000), Dev B (1500), Dev C (2500), Dev D (1000). How many times more lines did Dev C write than Dev D?

A bar graph displays server CPU utilization (%) from Monday to Friday: Mon (60%), Tue (70%), Wed (50%), Thu (80%), Fri (75%). What was the percentage increase in CPU utilization from Wednesday to Thursday?

A bar graph illustrates hourly transactions processed by four payment gateways: Gateway P (1200), Gateway Q (1800), Gateway R (900), Gateway S (1500). What is the total number of transactions processed by gateways handling more than 1000 transactions/hour?
A bar graph shows RAM usage (in GB) for four virtual machines: VM1 (16 GB), VM2 (8 GB), VM3 (32 GB), VM4 (12 GB). What is the total RAM if you provisioned two VM1s and one VM3?

A bar graph displays hourly requests handled by five microservices: Service A (310), Service B (400), Service C (250), Service D (350), Service E (200). Which service's request count is closest to the average?

A bar graph shows object storage usage (in TB) for four projects: Project K (2 TB), Project L (3 TB), Project M (1.5 TB), Project N (2.5 TB). If storage costs $50 per TB per month, what is the total monthly storage cost for all projects?

A bar graph illustrates the processing time (in seconds) for four data pipelines: Pipe A (180s), Pipe B (240s), Pipe C (120s), Pipe D (60s). What percentage of the total processing time does Pipe B account for?

A bar graph shows the number of open tickets for four support teams: Team 1 (85), Team 2 (60), Team 3 (105), Team 4 (70). What is the difference in open tickets between the team with the most and the second most tickets?

A bar graph displays the maximum concurrent users for four applications: App A (500), App B (750), App C (300), App D (650). If each server can handle 250 users, how many servers are needed to host App B and App D together?

A bar graph shows code efficiency scores (out of 100) for five software modules: M1 (85), M2 (78), M3 (92), M4 (75), M5 (88). If efficiency below 80 requires refactoring, which module has the lowest efficiency score among those needing refactoring?
A bar graph illustrates cloud resource consumption (in units) over four months: Jan (300), Feb (350), Mar (280), Apr (320). If each unit costs $1.50, what is the total cost for these four months?

A bar graph shows the number of tasks completed by five development teams in a sprint: Team A (12), Team B (18), Team C (15), Team D (10), Team E (20). What percentage of total tasks did Team C complete?
A bar graph depicts the number of repositories using specific programming languages: Python (150), Java (120), JavaScript (180), Go (90). Which language is used in the most repositories?

A bar graph shows server load (in requests/second) for four servers: Server 1 (120), Server 2 (90), Server 3 (150), Server 4 (110). If the critical load is 130 requests/second, what is the total excess load on servers exceeding this threshold?

A bar graph displays compile times (in minutes) for four different codebases: Codebase P (10 min), Codebase Q (15 min), Codebase R (8 min), Codebase S (13 min). What is the average compile time for these codebases?

A bar graph illustrates the number of successful deployments for four environments: Dev (40), Test (30), Staging (20), Prod (10). What percentage of all deployments were successful in the Production environment?

A bar graph shows monthly cloud expenses (in $) for four departments: Engineering (1500), Marketing (800), Sales (1200), Support (700). If Engineering migrates half its services to on-premise, reducing its cost by 40%, what is the new total monthly expense for all departments?

A bar graph shows current resource units for four projects: P1 (50), P2 (70), P3 (40), P4 (60). If P1 and P3 are scaled by 50% due to new features, which project then requires the most resource units?

A bar graph displays active user sessions (in thousands) for five regional servers: East (120), West (150), North (90), South (130), Central (110). What is the total number of sessions for servers with more than 100,000 active users?

A bar graph shows feature completion rates (%) for three releases: Release 1 (85%), Release 2 (92%), Release 3 (78%). What is the difference in completion rate between the highest and lowest performing release?

A bar graph illustrates hourly network bandwidth usage (in Mbps) for four applications: App A (250), App B (300), App C (180), App D (270). What is the average hourly bandwidth usage across all applications?

A line chart tracks database connection pool usage. What was the peak usage if it ranged from 10 to 80 connections over a day?

A line chart shows user logins increasing by 100 per hour. If there are 500 logins at 8 AM, how many will there be at 10 AM?
A line chart displays two lines: CPU temperature and room temperature. If CPU was 70°C and room was 25°C, what is their difference?
